_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning that verbs are connected to time — they can be in the past, present or future tense.

_source.w: We are learning that verbs are connected to time — they can be in the past, present or future tense.
y: Using the right tense tells the reader when something happened, is happening, or will happen.
Consistent tense use keeps our writing clear and easy to follow.
Understanding tense is essential for recounts, narratives and reports.
eg: We identify whether a sentence is in the past, present or future tense.
We change a sentence from past to present tense and notice how the verb changes.
We check our writing to make sure we have used a consistent tense throughout.
We notice that recounts use past tense while instructions use present tense.
We sort verb cards into past, present and future groups.
We write three versions of the same event — one in each tense — and compare them.
